name: vmaf_tiny_saliency_student_v2
notes: Fork-trained tiny U-Net (~124K params) trained from scratch on DUTS-TR (Wang et al. 2017, free for academic research). v2 of saliency_student — single-variable architectural ablation against v1: decoder upsampler swapped from nn.ConvTranspose2d(k=2,s=2) to F.interpolate(scale=2, bilinear, align_corners=False) followed by nn.Conv2d(k=3) (the standard 'Resize+Conv' U-Net upsampling pattern, admitted to the op-allowlist by ADR-0258). Encoder backbone, channel widths, skip connections, BCE+Dice loss, Adam lr=1e-3, CosineAnnealingLR, 50 epochs, batch=32, crop=256, seed=42, DUTS-TR corpus, 5% held-out validation fold all held identical to v1. Same I/O contract as v1 / mobilesal_placeholder_v0: ImageNet-normalised RGB NCHW [1,3,H,W] -> saliency map NCHW [1,1,H,W] in [0,1]. Gate PASSED: best val IoU = 0.7105 (v2) vs 0.6558 (v1) — +0.0547 (+8.3%). Ships as a parallel artefact under model/tiny/; v1 stays as production weights for the C-side mobilesal extractor until a follow-up PR validates v2 in real ROI encodes. DUTS images not redistributed in-tree; only the trained weights are. See docs/ai/models/saliency_student_v2.md and docs/adr/0332-saliency-student-v2-resize-decoder.md.

training.decoder_upsampler: F.interpolate(scale_factor=2.0, mode='bilinear', align_corners=False) + nn.Conv2d(kernel=3, padding=1, no bias)
